# RAYD SSL Certificates

`install.sh` automatically generates a private CA and a CA-signed server certificate.
Once clients trust the CA, HTTPS opens without any browser warnings.

## Files generated by install.sh

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `rayd-ca.key` | CA private key — **keep secret, never share** |
| `rayd-ca.crt` | CA certificate — **distribute to all client PCs** |
| `fullchain.pem` | Server certificate (signed by the CA) — used by nginx |
| `privkey.pem` | Server private key — used by nginx |

---

## Client setup — install the CA certificate once per PC

### Windows (run as Administrator)
```bat
certutil -addstore Root nginx\certs\rayd-ca.crt
```
Or: double-click `rayd-ca.crt` → **Install Certificate** → **Local Machine**
→ **Place all certificates in the following store** → **Trusted Root Certification Authorities**

Restart Chrome/Edge after installing.

### Linux (Ubuntu/Debian)
```bash
sudo cp nginx/certs/rayd-ca.crt /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/rayd-ca.crt
sudo update-ca-certificates
```

### macOS
```bash
sudo security add-trusted-cert -d -r trustRoot \
  -k /Library/Keychains/System.keychain nginx/certs/rayd-ca.crt
```

---

## Regenerating the server certificate (e.g. hostname changed)

Delete `fullchain.pem` and `privkey.pem`, then re-run `install.sh`.
The existing CA will be reused — clients don't need to reinstall anything.

```bash
rm nginx/certs/fullchain.pem nginx/certs/privkey.pem
sudo bash install.sh
```

## Renewing before expiry (825-day validity)

Same as regenerating — delete the server cert files and re-run `install.sh`.
The CA itself is valid for 10 years.
